reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own answer (96 - 58 = 38) is solid, and in all previous games the others’ simple arithmetic has been highly reliable, with only about one wrong answer across many puzzles. That makes the chance that any of Q2–Q4 is incorrect likely much lower than the 25% random-death risk if nobody volunteers this round (and historically B, C, and D almost never volunteer first).  

Verifying a question would still leave me exposed to that same 25% random-elimination risk this round, while also costing 1 coin, so it doesn’t improve my overall survival odds. Volunteering immediately with the current capsules maximizes my chance of living and lets me keep all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: All four answers are currently unverified. Volunteering now would mean trusting three unchecked capsules (Q1, Q3, Q4), which is unnecessary risk even though I know my own Q2 (85 - 47) is correct. If no one volunteers this round, a random player dies whether I pass or verify, so my survival odds this round are the same either way. Verifying Q1 instead of passing strictly improves our information for later rounds (building early consensus on Q1) at the same survival risk, so I’ll spend 1 coin to verify Q1.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING:  
Volunteering in Round 1 would mean staking my life on three unverified answers (especially D’s Q4), which is unnecessary personal risk when someone else is very likely to step up. Across recent games the pattern has been: B verifies Q1-A, then A volunteers immediately and enters the correct password; D has also been consistently correct lately, lowering (but not eliminating) concern about Q4.

Given that, verifying is still dominated by passing from a self‑interested perspective:

- If A volunteers this round as usual and is correct, the game ends immediately; any verification I did would just burn 1 coin for no benefit.
- If, unexpectedly, nobody volunteers and we move to Round 2, my random‑death risk is the same whether I verified or passed this round, but verifying would already have cost me a coin. I can always choose to verify later if the game continues.
- I already know my own puzzle (Q3: 49+33=82) is correct, so verifying Q3 adds no value.

So the best move is to keep all 4 coins, avoid the volunteer’s death risk, and wait to see what others do.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+B (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+B, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
